defs: The same; the selfsame; the very same; not different; as, the identical person or thing.
quotes: I can not remember a thing that happened a year ago, without a conviction . . . that I, the same identical person who now remember that event, did then exist.

defs: Uttering sameness or the same truth; expressing in the predicate what is given, or obviously implied, in the subject; tautological.
quotes: When you say body is solid, I say that you make an identical proposition, because it is impossible to have the idea of body without that of solidity.

defs: In diplomacy (esp. in the form identic), precisely agreeing in sentiment or opinion and form or manner of expression; -- applied to concerted action or language which is used by two or more governments in treating with another government.
